title: The Macro Minute: Which is a bigger upside risk—US-China trade, the “Shadow Fed”, or Musk kowtowing?

Darius Dell to present our macro minute
for Wednesday June 11th 2025. As always
we'll start with the executive summary
from today's lead up morninging note. If
you would like to review the analysis
supporting these conclusions and to see
if you need to make any changes in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
market risk or to get on the right side
of market risk to the extent you haven't
been following our kiss and Dr. Mo
signals need to be a client of 42 macro.
So today's key macro question, which is
a bigger upside risk for asset markets,
the US China trade negotiations, the
advent of a shadow Fed or Elon Musk
cowttowing to President Trump? Our
answer, the US and China established a
framework to implement the Geneva
consensus and revive sensitive goods
trade flows after two days of high
stakes talks in London. The framework
pending approval from presidents Donald
Trump and Xi Jinping includes China
expediting rare earth shipments in the
US loosening export controls on select
high-tech products. Wendy Cutler, a
former US trade negotiator and Asian
Society Policy Institute vice president,
called the US move to ease export
controls unprecedented.
While unprecedented may be correct, it
was not unexpected for anyone paying
attention to our research over the past
two months. Despite lingering trade
policy challenges, a Goldman Sachs
basket of the most shorted US stocks
rallied 9% over five days through
Tuesday and has surged 16% since late
May. This is clear evidence of the
upside capulation we have persistently
been warning about in our research.
Elsewhere in Parad Paradigm C news,
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessett is
reported to be in line to replace Jerome
Pow as Fed chair. Other contenders
allegedly include Fed former Fed
Governor Kev Kevin Kevin Walsh, White
House National Economic uh Council
Director Kevin Hasset, Fed Governor
Chris Waller, and former World Bank
President David Malpass. Trump's pick
for Fed chair and waiting is widely
expected to favor lower interest rates
amid incrementally disisconfirming
evidence of our sticky inflation theme
for the May CBI data. Lastly, Tesla's
leading Mac 7 gains after Elon Musk
expressed regret over his Trump related
post and confirmed a June 22nd target
for the robo taxi network launch. The
billionaires at top our K-shaped economy
know exactly where their bread is
buttered, i.e. in the 37% of 7 trillion
plus in annual federal government
expenditures that are not earmarked for
social security, Medicare or poor
people. The reason we have a K-shaped
society is because executives at
companies like Tesla, SpaceX, Lockheed,
Barton, RTX, General Dynamics, Boeing,
Palanteer, etc. all have priority access
to the federal government feeding trough
and the fact that the tax code unfairly
prioritizes capital income over wage
income. For example, you got the carried
interest loophole deductions, all that
stuff, the qualified business
deductions, the 1031 exchanges and real
estate, forgivable PPP loans, etc. The
government is consistently handing out
tens and hundreds of billions of dollars
to rich people year after year after
year. Their constituents proximity to
these unfair benefits, yet tax cuts and
and and expenditures is why
congressional Republicans in the Trump
administration are failing on their
promise to dramatically shrink the size
of the federal government. Long live
paradigm C. transitioning to our 42 mac.

there? uh social supplemented security

annualized year-to-ate basis, on an
annualized basis here in 2025, the
government is on track to spend about
$1.3 trillion on poor people. They're on
track to spend about $3.2 trillion on
Social Security and Medicaid. And guess
what the rest of the money is being
spent on? It's everything else. That's
$2.6 trillion. It's 2x the amount the
government spends on poor people that

else. Now, what's everything else? I
don't know. Defense contract
procurement. I don't know. Uh uh chip
act subsidies. I don't know. Inflation
reduction act subsidies. I don't know.

all there's all this stuff that keeps
going to billionaires in Silicon Valley
and on Wall Street and across America.
That's $2.6 trillion. And you want to
know why they won't shrink the federal
government? Because these are their
constituents.
It's the size of the federal government
is not going to go down in any
meaningful way in this administration or
any subsequent administration as a
function of this. And so you as an
investor need to understand that the
government's
subsidizing of rich people in this
country is why we have a K-shaped
economy in the first place. And
ultimately it's likely to persist. And
as long as that K-shaped economy
persists, the more likely it is that the
traditional defensive assets like
treasury bonds and the dollar are no
longer defensive. The defensive assets
in in paradigm C are stocks, gold, and
Bitcoin. That's playing defense until
they get rid of this what I would
consider to be gross dynamic
spending 2x the amount of money on
everything else that we are on poor
people. This is the money that goes into
Chimoth's pocket. This is the money that
goes into Elon's pocket. This is the
money that goes into Alex Karp's pocket.
This is the money that goes into every
poor person in America's pocket. It's
half of that. And so until we fix this
what I would consider to be gross
dynamic, we're going to maintain the
K-shaped economy. And the big the top
part of the K is way bigger than the
bottom part of the K. That's the United
States economy in a nutshell. Folks, I'm
not telling you that it's I'm telling
you that it's not good. And this is why
we're going to have a deepening of the
four turning poly crisis. Everything
I've been talking about for the last 5
10 minutes. This is why we're having a
deepening fourth turning poly crisis.
The inequality is the reason of the
forturning. The people on the bottom
part of the K get fed up enough that
they start to create political
volatility and they're willing to go to
war to fix it and they will fix it. And
the path to getting there is not going
to be great. But as long as you're long
defensive assets like stocks, gold, and
Bitcoin, and Paradigm C, you're going to
do just fine if your goal is to retire
on time and comfortably. So,
transitioning to our uh 42 micro

the next crypto bare market, whether
that's a crypto winter or just a 20, 30,
40% decline in in in the Bitcoin price.
I mean, that's exactly what KISS does.
KISS takes our market regime now casting
process signals and tells you, hey, it's
risk on, so be long Bitcoin. And when
it's time to say, hey, it's risk off
now. Stop being long Bitcoin. take your
exposure down and raise cash so you can
eliminate volatility drag for your
portfolio and start compounding returns
for the next market cycle from a higher
net asset value than the fools who are
hodddling all the way down to the lows

were choosing to suffer volatility drag

has comment. Sorry, that's the reply. So
my my my question my response to this is
I have no idea if Bitcoin winters are
canceled. I don't know how anybody with

straight face. This is not analysis.
That's just an opinion. And we don't

people's opinions here on this program.
Uh the one thing I will say is um if you
look at uh one of my favorite um quotes
from one of my favorite books um this
actually is my second favorite my
favorite finance book is my second
favorite book of all time behind the
holy bible is thinking fast and slow and
this is one of my favorite quotes from
that book which is um subjective
confidence in a judgment is not a
reasoned evaluation of the probability
that this judgment is correct.
Confidence is a feeling which reflects
the coherence of the information and the
cognitive ease of processing. It is wise
to take admissions of uncertainty
serious, but declarations of high
confidence mainly tell you that an
individual has constructed a coherent
story in his mind, Mr. Sailor, not
necessarily that the story is true. Now,
again, I think Michael Sailor is one of
the smartest human beings, one of the
greatest financial engineers in the
history of mankind, brilliant man, one
of the smartest people in the world. But
the smartest person in the world knows
doesn't know anything about the future
more doesn't know anything more about
the future than the squirrel that I'm
looking at right now. That squirrel
knows just a much just as much about the
future as I do and Michael Salem because
no one knows anything about the future.
The future is inherently unknowable. All
we're doing is trying to assess the
distribution of probable outcomes and
how that distribution is changing. Well,
that's what the institutional vessels
are doing. I have no idea what folks who
don't have these um skills are doing,
but that's neither here nor there. Um
that's so uh the one thing I'll say on
all this we wrap up is that your goal as
an investor shouldn't be trying to you
know create these high highly certain
high confidence outcomes in your head.
Your goal as an investor is to just
dispassionately manage risk whenever
it's time to manage risk. And if there's
not time to manage risk then you need to
be fully invested. And guess what?
Stocks, gold, and Bitcoin. That's Kiss.
Have a great day out there, folks. We'll
